description_long: This object is a traditional banjo, featuring a circular drum-like body with a taut, light-colored membrane stretched across the top, surrounded by a series of metal brackets. The neck is long and slender, made of wood, and is adorned with dark fret markers and metallic tuning pegs at the headstock. The headstock is ornately carved and holds four visible tuning pegs, with a fifth peg located partway down the neck, characteristic of classic five-string banjos. The body rim is metallic, giving the instrument a robust and slightly industrial appearance, while the back of the body is solid and dark. The strings run from the tailpiece at the base, over a floating bridge, and up the neck to the headstock. This banjo is well-suited for use in urban parks, street performances, or music venues.
description: A classic five-string banjo with a circular drum body, metallic rim, and a long wooden neck featuring ornate headstock and tuning pegs.
description_view_0: The banjo is seen at an angle, highlighting the circular body, metallic rim, and the full length of the wooden neck.
description_view_1: This view shows the front of the banjo, displaying the taut membrane, bridge, strings, and detailed fretboard.
description_view_2: The back of the banjo is visible, showing the solid dark back and the wooden neck extending upward.
description_view_3: A rear angled view reveals the back of the body, the neck, and the headstock with tuning pegs.
attribute_headstock_design: Ornately carved, four tuning pegs on headstock, one side peg on neck, no visible logo
